key_insight: Anisotropic short-range chemical order in wurtzite ScAlN—not just composition—raises the intrinsic ferroelectric switching barrier by reorganizing local polar connectivity.
engineering_relevance: Controlling local alloy ordering offers a new materials-engineering lever to tune ferroelectric switching energy in ScAlN without changing overall composition.
method: First-principles canonical sampling of wurtzite ScAlN alloys
Comparison of short-range-ordered versus random-alloy configurations
Motif-resolved analysis of local Sc–N–Sc and Sc–N–Al–N–Sc structures
evidence_signal: Across a broad composition range, the simulations show that SRO systematically increases switching barriers and that columnar Sc–N–Al–N–Sc motifs best correlate with barrier variation.
watch_item: The conclusions are based on atomistic simulation and motif statistics, so experimental confirmation of achievable SRO states and their stability will be important.
paper_title: Anisotropic Short-Range Order Modulates Ferroelectric Switching in Wurtzite ScAlN Alloys
